message:
fix: bound poll/select/epoll/timerfd buffers via tracked-region check

Extends the "bound X buffers" series (a10f7840 io, 01ec384c socket,
…) into the poll/select/epoll/timerfd surface so the rest of
poll_abi enforces the same EFAULT-before-syscall contract as
read/write. Previously these entry points handed user-supplied
struct pointers — pollfd arrays, fd_sets, timespec, sigset_t,
epoll_event arrays, itimerspec — straight to the kernel; a tracked
allocation shorter than the structure size would page-fault inside
the syscall instead of getting rejected at the libc shim.

Helpers (file-private):

* tracked_region_fits(ptr, len) — len-aware version of the io_internal
  helper that accepts zero-len + null ptrs. The 0-byte case bypasses
  pointer checks so callers like ppoll's "no sigmask" branch don't
  spuriously fail.
* tracked_array_fits<T>(ptr, count) — multiplies size_of::<T>()
  with count using checked_mul to guard the overflow case where
  count is large enough to wrap usize, then delegates to
  tracked_region_fits.
* tracked_object_fits<T>(ptr) — convenience for single-element
  structs (pollfd, epoll_event, itimerspec, ulong sigset).
* tracked_optional_object_fits<T>(ptr) — same but accepts null
  (used for "optional out parameter" arguments like select() timeout
  and timerfd_settime() old_value).
* select_fd_set_bytes(nfds) — computes the actual fd_set size
  glibc would use for a given nfds: ceil(nfds / fd_mask_bits) *
  size_of::<c_long>(). This is narrower than the static
  size_of::<libc::fd_set>() (128 bytes), so callers that allocated
  exactly the bytes they need still pass the check.
* tracked_fd_set_fits — null-permissive wrapper around the above.
* reject_efault() — common one-liner that sets EFAULT and returns
  -1, since five entry points share the failure path.

Wired guards:

* poll() — fds array of actual_nfds pollfds.
* ppoll() — fds + optional timespec + 8-byte sigset_t when sigmask
  is non-null.
* select() — three fd_sets sized for actual_nfds + optional
  timeval. Each fd_set check gates on null first so callers passing
  one or two of the three sets still proceed.
* pselect() — same as select plus 8-byte sigset_t.
* epoll_ctl() — event struct (one epoll_event), but only when op is
  ADD or MOD; DEL ignores the event pointer per the kernel contract,
  so the guard is also gated on op.
* epoll_wait() — events array sized for maxevents.
* epoll_pwait() — events array + 8-byte sigset_t when present.
* timerfd_settime() — required new_value itimerspec + optional
  old_value out itimerspec.
* timerfd_gettime() — required curr_value out itimerspec.

Each rejected call also emits a runtime_policy::observe with the
adverse=true flag so policy meters count the failed transfer
under the appropriate ApiFamily (Poll for poll/select/epoll,
Time for timerfd) — keeps observability consistent with the
non-EFAULT failure paths above each guard.
